featured_objects.name: Moon
featured_objects.description: Earth's only natural satellite, visible with the naked eye and full of craters, maria, and mountain ranges.
interesting_facts: The Moon shows the same face to Earth because it is tidally locked.
Its phases are caused by how much sunlight we can see from Earth.
featured_objects.ai_prompt: Explain the Moon in beginner-friendly space terms.

featured_objects.name: Sun
featured_objects.description: The Sun is the star at the center of our solar system. It gives Earth light, heat, and energy.
interesting_facts: The Sun contains more than 99% of the solar system's mass.
Its surface temperature is about 5,500°C.
featured_objects.ai_prompt: Explain the Sun in simple space language.

featured_objects.name: Jupiter
featured_objects.description: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system. It is famous for its Great Red Spot and many moons.
interesting_facts: Jupiter could fit more than 1,300 Earths inside it.
Its day is very short because it spins quickly.
featured_objects.ai_prompt: Explain Jupiter for a class 7 student.

featured_objects.name: Mars
featured_objects.description: Mars is the Red Planet. Its dusty surface looks red because of iron oxide, and it is a major target for exploration.
interesting_facts: Mars has the tallest volcano in the solar system, Olympus Mons.
Robots like Curiosity and Perseverance have explored Mars.
featured_objects.ai_prompt: Explain Mars and why it matters to space exploration.

featured_objects.name: Betelgeuse
featured_objects.description: Betelgeuse is a huge red star in Orion. It is easy to spot because of its orange-red color.
interesting_facts: It is much larger than the Sun.
Astronomers watch it closely because it is nearing the end of its life.

featured_objects.name: Sirius
featured_objects.description: Sirius is the brightest star in Earth's night sky. It is part of the Canis Major constellation.
interesting_facts: Sirius is actually a star system with two stars.
It was important in many ancient calendars.

featured_objects.name: Andromeda Galaxy
featured_objects.description: The Andromeda Galaxy is our nearest large galaxy neighbor and is slowly moving toward the Milky Way.
interesting_facts: It can sometimes be seen with the naked eye from dark locations.
Andromeda and the Milky Way may merge far in the future.

featured_objects.name: Milky Way
featured_objects.description: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains Earth, the Sun, and our solar system.
interesting_facts: We see it as a bright band across the sky on dark nights.
It has hundreds of billions of stars.

featured_objects.name: Orion Nebula
featured_objects.description: The Orion Nebula is a bright cloud of gas and dust where new stars are forming.
interesting_facts: It is one of the easiest nebulae to observe with binoculars.
It sits inside the Orion constellation.

featured_objects.name: Pleiades
featured_objects.description: The Pleiades is a famous star cluster that looks like a tiny dipper of bright stars.
interesting_facts: It is also called the Seven Sisters.
Many cultures have stories about these stars.

featured_objects.name: Orion
featured_objects.description: Orion is a famous winter constellation with a recognizable belt of three bright stars.
interesting_facts: It contains Betelgeuse and Rigel.
Orion is easy for beginners to find in the night sky.

featured_objects.name: Ursa Major
featured_objects.description: Ursa Major is a big constellation that includes the Big Dipper asterism.
interesting_facts: It helps stargazers find the North Star.
Its shape is one of the easiest to recognize.
featured_objects.ai_prompt: Explain Ursa Major and the Big Dipper.

featured_objects.name: International Space Station
featured_objects.description: The ISS is a large research station orbiting Earth where astronauts live and work.
interesting_facts: It travels around Earth in about 90 minutes.
It is visible from the ground as a moving bright point.

featured_objects.name: Starlink Satellite Train
featured_objects.description: Starlink satellites often appear as a moving line of lights soon after launch.
interesting_facts: They support internet connectivity from orbit.
Their visibility changes after deployment.

featured_objects.name: Perseid Meteors
featured_objects.description: The Perseids are a famous meteor shower caused by debris from Comet Swift-Tuttle.
interesting_facts: Meteor showers are best seen away from city lights.
Meteors are tiny space rocks that glow as they burn.

featured_objects.name: Deep Sky Objects
featured_objects.description: Deep sky objects include galaxies, nebulae, clusters, and other faint targets viewed through telescopes.
interesting_facts: Many need binoculars or telescopes to see clearly.
They are a favorite target for astrophotography.

control_actions.label: Current Location
control_actions.query: Show me the sky from my current location.

control_actions.label: Tonight's Sky
control_actions.query: What should I look for in tonight's sky?

control_actions.label: Telescope Mode
control_actions.query: Help me switch to telescope mode.
